items.caption: Tilting disc inconel check valve . Valves and are generally made of plastic or steel, the latter being the most widely used in chemical and petrochemical applications. Steel grades range from the most common type, namely carbon steel, with standard stainless steel being the second most common. Stainless steel alloys, which include nickel or copper are used in applications that require higher corrosion or heat resistance. In such cases, the most commonly used valve configurations -- ball valves, gate valves, check valves, globe valves and butterfly valves -- are often forged or cast as duplex valves, super duplex valves, alloy 20 valves, monel valves, inconel valves, incoloy valves and 254 SMO valves (6Mo valves). Titanium valves are also used in some highly corrosive applications. Titanium is not a stainless steel alloy.

A stainless steel dual plate check valve against a white background. the valve has a flanged connection and is designed for use in industrial piping systems. the valve is shown in two positions: one with the valve open and the other with the valve closed. the valve is marked with the text "316 stainless steel dual plate wafer type check valve," indicating the material and type of the valve. a product photograph, likely intended for a catalog or online store, showcasing the valve's design and features.
